mechanism_narrative: Persistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals strong labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >60% of periods when PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k/mon, or the Rank IC falls below 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 3.0% indicates labor market tightness that accelerates wage inflation, prompting central bank rate hikes and negative SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE declines below 3.0%, or the signal's t-statistic < 1.96 in 2-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3.5%) and declines in federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 4.5%) create a 'stagflation' environment where equity valuations contract, producing negative SPX returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >60% of triggered periods, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 5.0%) raises discount rates on future cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days during active rate hiking cycles.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in >60% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the signal's Sharpe ratio < 0.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3.0% indicates robust demand conditions that enhance corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in >60% of periods when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0%, or the signal's IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100.0 indicates weakening supply chain capacity, causing inventory shortages and negative SPX returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in >60% of instances when INDPRO declines below 100.0, or the signal's t-statistic < 1.96 in 2-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST > 1.6M units) and retail sales (RSAFS > 680B) signals strong consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in >60% of triggered periods, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in 3-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in consumer confidence (RSAFS < 680B) indicates weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days during economic slowdowns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in >60% of instances when RSAFS declines below 680B, or the signal's IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.
